steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: The cruising speed s varies between 135 mph and 160 mph. 'Between' with stated endpoints means s is at least 135 and at most 160.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: This is a compound inequality: $135 \leq s \leq 160$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: All four answer choices are simple or compound inequalities; only one captures both bounds simultaneously.

explanation:
• Choice A — ‘$s \leq 25$’: 25 = 160 - 135, the range width, but the speed itself is not bounded by 25 mph — it is bounded between 135 and 160. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘$s \leq 135$’: Only sets an upper bound of 135, allowing speeds below the minimum of 135 mph, and doesn't capture the maximum of 160. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘$s \leq 160$’: Only sets an upper bound of 160, allowing speeds from 0 up to 160, which misses the minimum constraint of 135. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘$135 \leq s \leq 160$’: Correctly captures both that the speed is at least 135 mph and at most 160 mph. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
